description: Reusable one-task end-to-end Legion coding benchmark. Scripted modes prove the evaluator; the live legion-fanout-review mode proves one coding task can route, fan out, apply code, review, evaluate, emit observability HTML, and record self-learn data.

modes.label: No-op scripted baseline
modes.description: Leaves the stub in place. Used to prove the evaluator fails bad implementations without model spend.

modes.label: Scripted oracle
modes.description: Applies answer_files. Used to prove the evaluator passes the intended solution without model spend.

modes.label: Legion fanout + review
modes.description: Runs one task through route, legion-fanout, diff apply, legion-delegate final-review, golden eval, observability HTML, share metrics, self-learn, heal, and the nested core bench.

cases.summary: Implement a deterministic FieldOps ticket triage scorer and pass a golden eval set.
cases.task: Edit fieldops_triage.py only. Implement triage_ticket(message, inventory=None) so python3 eval_fieldops_triage.py passes. Return a dict with category, urgency, required_skill, required_parts, risk_score, and customer_eta_required. Treat status phrases like still cold as urgency hints, not as refrigeration category by themselves. Do not edit eval_fieldops_triage.py.

files.eval_fieldops_triage.py:
import json
from fieldops_triage import triage_ticket

CASES = [
    {
        "message": "Our cafe freezer is down and we are losing stock. Can someone come today?",
        "inventory": {"compressor": 1, "fan_motor": 0, "filter": 4},
        "category": "refrigeration",
        "urgency": "emergency",
        "required_skill": "refrigeration",
        "part": "compressor",
        "risk_min": 90,
        "risk_max": 100,
        "eta": True,
    },
    {
        "message": "No heat in the daycare classroom and toddlers arrive in an hour.",
        "inventory": {"thermostat": 2, "filter": 3},
        "category": "hvac",
        "urgency": "emergency",
        "required_skill": "hvac",
        "part": "thermostat",
        "risk_min": 85,
        "risk_max": 100,
        "eta": True,
    },
    {
        "message": "No heat in the classroom but the room is still cold.",
        "inventory": {"thermostat": 1, "fan_motor": 2},
        "category": "hvac",
        "urgency": "same_day",
        "required_skill": "hvac",
        "part": "thermostat",
        "risk_min": 50,
        "risk_max": 85,
        "eta": True,
    },
    {
        "message": "The kitchen sink is draining slowly. Next week is fine.",
        "inventory": {"trap": 2},
        "category": "plumbing",
        "urgency": "scheduled",
        "required_skill": "plumbing",
        "part": "trap",
        "risk_min": 0,
        "risk_max": 45,
        "eta": False,
    },
    {
        "message": "Breaker keeps tripping and the lights flicker in the stock room.",
        "inventory": {"breaker": 1},
        "category": "electrical",
        "urgency": "same_day",
        "required_skill": "electrical",
        "part": "breaker",
        "risk_min": 60,
        "risk_max": 89,
        "eta": True,
    },
    {
        "message": "Walk-in cooler is making a loud noise but it is still cold.",
        "inventory": {"fan_motor": 1, "compressor": 0},
        "category": "refrigeration",
        "urgency": "same_day",
        "required_skill": "refrigeration",
        "part": "fan_motor",
        "risk_min": 50,
        "risk_max": 80,
        "eta": True,
    },
    {
        "message": "Annual HVAC filter service for the office, no rush.",
        "inventory": {"filter": 8},
        "category": "hvac",
        "urgency": "scheduled",
        "required_skill": "hvac",
        "part": "filter",
        "risk_min": 0,
        "risk_max": 35,
        "eta": False,
    },
]


def check_case(case):
    result = triage_ticket(case["message"], inventory=case["inventory"])
    if not isinstance(result, dict):
        return ["result is not a dict"]
    failures = []
    for key in ("category", "urgency", "required_skill", "required_parts", "risk_score", "customer_eta_required"):
        if key not in result:
            failures.append(f"missing {key}")
    if failures:
        return failures
    if result["category"] != case["category"]:
        failures.append(f"category expected {case['category']} got {result['category']}")
    if result["urgency"] != case["urgency"]:
        failures.append(f"urgency expected {case['urgency']} got {result['urgency']}")
    if result["required_skill"] != case["required_skill"]:
        failures.append(f"skill expected {case['required_skill']} got {result['required_skill']}")
    parts = result["required_parts"]
    if not isinstance(parts, list) or case["part"] not in parts:
        failures.append(f"required_parts missing {case['part']}: {parts}")
    risk = result["risk_score"]
    if not isinstance(risk, int) or not (case["risk_min"] <= risk <= case["risk_max"]):
        failures.append(f"risk_score expected {case['risk_min']}..{case['risk_max']} got {risk}")
    if result["customer_eta_required"] is not case["eta"]:
        failures.append(f"eta expected {case['eta']} got {result['customer_eta_required']}")
    return failures


def main():
    failures = {case["message"]: check_case(case) for case in CASES}
    failures = {message: items for message, items in failures.items() if items}
    total = len(CASES)
    passed = total - len(failures)
    payload = {"schema": "fieldops.triage.eval.v1", "passed": passed == total, "score": passed, "total": total, "failures": failures}
    with open("score.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
        json.dump(payload, handle, indent=2, sort_keys=True)
        handle.write("\n")
    print(json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True))
    if failures:
        raise SystemExit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
